title: penstemon penlandii
intro: Penstemon penlandii, also known as Penland beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant in the penstemon family that is native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Penstemon penlandii include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Penstemon penlandii can be prepared as a tea or tincture by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, and it can also be used topically as a salve or poultice to apply directly to affected areas.
